rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM MECHLING VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment Entered Against Associated Insulation Of California, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for August 11, 2016, Thursday, in Department 503, Line 1. Fireman's Fund Insurance Company's motion to set aside entry of default and default judgment is granted. Fireman's Fund is allowed to bring said motion. (See Western Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 1196.) The default entered against defendant Associated Insulation of California on June 1, 2010 and the amended default entered March 19, 2012 and the default judgment entered against defendant Associated Insulation of California on July 9, 2015 are set aside pursuant to the court's inherent, equitable power to set aside a default on the ground of extrinsic fraud or mistake. Plaintiff's request for a continuance is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: LARRY WEAVER VS. GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for August 11, 2016, Thursday, in Department 503, Line 6. Brayton Purcell LLP,'s, Nancy Williams' motion to be relieved as counsel for plaintiffs Larry Weaver and Nancy Weaver is granted. Brayton Purcell demonstrated they properly served plaintiffs. No opposition filed. Hearing required unless Brayton Purcell informs plaintiffs of the tentative ruling and they state that they do not wish to contest. The hearing will be at 9:30a.m. Brayton Purcell must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN F. MILLS VS. CRANE CO. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for August 11, 2016, Thursday, in Department 503, Line 7. Defendant B.R. Funsten & Co.'s demurrer to plaintiff's first amended complaint is overruled. Pursuant to plaintiff's opposition, the Court disregards the demurrer due to defendant's violations of C.C.P. § 430.60 and CRC Rules 3.1112 and 3.1320. Defendant has 10 days to answer. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)
